title: Clock, Delta Time, Fixed Steps, Interpolation, and Stalls · Runtime Lens: Time Evidence
summary: Movement is tuned per frame, a long pause launches the ship across the map, collision results change with monitor refresh rate, and catch-up updates freeze the process. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the time evidence increment in bounded deterministic time-step policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: Replay identical inputs across 30, 60, 144, jittered, paused, and stalled presentation traces and compare state tolerances.
Clamp pathological frame time and cap catch-up updates while recording discarded debt and player impact.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-time-evidence — Replay identical inputs across 30, 60, 144, jittered, paused, and stalled presentation traces and compare state tolerances.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-time-step-simulation-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-time-step-simulation-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_time_step_simulation_2(frame_seconds: float = 0.016, max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1, fixed_step: float = 1 / 120, accumulator: float = 0.0, max_updates: int = 8) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if frame_seconds < 0.0 or fixed_step <= 0.0:
        return False, frame_seconds, "time-domain"
    clamped = min(frame_seconds, max_frame_seconds)
    updates = int((accumulator + clamped) / fixed_step)
    if updates > max_updates:
        return False, updates, "spiral-guard"
    if clamped > max_frame_seconds:
        return False, clamped, "clamp-failure"
    assert 0 <= updates <= max_updates
    return True, updates, "bounded-time-step"

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Time Evidence, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

Keep the Time Evidence artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the time evidence defect evidence boundary for Time Evidence.

For repair the time evidence defect, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Time Evidence for bounded deterministic time-step policy.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Time Evidence model · Theory Time Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Time Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 8508 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound delta time, fixed simulation updates, interpolation debt, and the spiral-of-death guard; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Replay identical inputs across 30, 60, 144, jittered, paused, and stalled presentation traces and compare state tolerances.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Time Evidence model · Theory Time Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Time Evidence through the concrete bounded deterministic time-step policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Time Evidence model · Theory Time Evidence, keep Time Evidence connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Replay identical inputs across 30, 60, 144, jittered, paused, and stalled presentation traces and compare state tolerances.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Time Evidence model · Theory Time Evidence, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Time Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Time Evidence model · Theory Time Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 8508 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound delta time, fixed simulation updates, interpolation debt, and the spiral-of-death guard; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Replay identical inputs across 30, 60, 144, jittered, paused, and stalled presentation traces and compare state tolerances.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Time Evidence model · Theory Time Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Average frames per second proves timing correctness.
